description: Classifies direct Redis enqueue call sites that are not statically proven task-outbox backed by scripts/operation-task-inventory.mjs.

classifications.reason: HTTP handlers persist the allowlist row and audit event before this best-effort on-demand reconcile. Missed delivery leaves durable desired state in Postgres.
classifications.validation: apiserver_allowlist:reconcile_all is registered and scheduled every 15 minutes to reconcile active allowlists.

classifications.reason: This endpoint is an explicit delivery diagnostic and does not commit product state. An enqueue failure is returned to the caller, while a successfully accepted notification task uses the normal dispatcher retries.
classifications.validation: NewNotificationSendTask places the task on the critical queue with MaxRetry(3). There is intentionally no durable delivery receipt or repair sweep for a test notification, so the operator must re-run a failed diagnostic.

classifications.reason: Registry config writes are durable in cluster_registry_configs; this direct enqueue only accelerates Secret/ServiceAccount convergence.
classifications.validation: cluster:registry_drift_reconcile is registered and scheduled every 30 minutes, and its handler re-applies every registry row idempotently.

classifications.reason: Production server wiring sets taskOutbox on ClusterTemplateHandler; the direct enqueue is a fallback only when the durable outbox is unavailable.
classifications.validation: internal/server/server.go wires SetTaskOutbox(queries), and cluster_template:drift_check is also scheduled on the tunnel queue for repair.

classifications.reason: The cluster and its label mutation are durable before this best-effort acceleration task. Missing direct delivery leaves the cluster eligible for the periodic selector-based auto-adoption reconciliation.
classifications.validation: argocd:auto_register_cluster is registered and scheduled every five minutes; its fleet sweep pages all clusters, re-evaluates the persisted selector and labels, and repairs managed-cluster state.

classifications.reason: Refreshing labels on an already-registered upstream Argo CD cluster Secret is metadata convergence, not the authoritative adoption state. A cluster update can re-issue the refresh.
classifications.validation: The durable managed-cluster records remain in Postgres; this task only refreshes upstream Secret labels.

classifications.reason: Cluster auto-attach writes durable cluster/template application state and production server wiring provides task_outbox. The direct enqueue is only the no-outbox fallback.
classifications.validation: ClusterTemplateHandler uses the same task_outbox helper, and cluster_template:drift_check repairs missed template application delivery.

classifications.reason: Notification fan-out is side-effect delivery for already-detected control-plane alerts. It should not block control-plane health responses.
classifications.validation: Failure only drops an outbound notification attempt; alert state remains queryable through control-plane status.

classifications.reason: Fleet template application first upserts cluster_template_applications; direct enqueue only accelerates work from the durable row.
classifications.validation: cluster_template:drift_check is registered on the tunnel queue and re-enqueues failed/stale application rows.

classifications.reason: Network policy desired state is persisted before enqueue; missed delivery is repaired by periodic apply and drift sweeps.
classifications.validation: network_policy:apply is scheduled every 5 minutes and network_policy:drift_check is scheduled every 30 minutes.

classifications.reason: The handler attempts task_outbox before the direct queue fallback, and the Reapply endpoint writes cluster_template_applications first.
classifications.validation: Production ClusterTemplateHandler server wiring sets taskOutbox and cluster_template:drift_check repairs missed direct delivery.

classifications.reason: The transactional namespace mutation persists the project_namespaces sidecar before dispatch. When no live requester is available, this path writes task_outbox with a stable project/cluster/namespace/op dedupe key before reaching the direct Redis fallback.
classifications.validation: Production server wiring calls SetTaskOutbox, dispatchProjectReconcile uses enqueueProjectTaskOutbox, and the existing project async tests assert the same helper's payload, queue, dedupe key, and delivery-attempt contract.

classifications.reason: Production server wiring provides a live requester, so project apply runs in-process. Non-requester fallback persists task_outbox before direct Redis enqueue, after project_namespaces is persisted.
classifications.validation: TestProjectHandlerUpsertAndEnqueueUsesTaskOutboxWithoutRequester asserts the fallback writes task_outbox with a stable dedupe key and project:reconcile payload.

classifications.reason: Production server wiring provides a live requester, so namespace cleanup runs in-process. Non-requester fallback now persists task_outbox before direct Redis enqueue; DB-only cleanup remains limited to no-outbox/no-queue dev wiring.
classifications.validation: TestProjectHandlerEnqueueCleanupUsesTaskOutboxWithoutRequester asserts cleanup writes task_outbox, keeps the DB sidecar row for task ownership, and stores the remove payload.

classifications.reason: This is a shared instrumentation wrapper around asynq.Client.Enqueue, not an independent product action producer.
classifications.validation: Callers remain responsible for their own durable intent or best-effort classification.

classifications.reason: The firing or resolved alert event is persisted before outbound channel fan-out, so alert state remains visible even when a notification enqueue fails. Channel failures are intentionally isolated so they cannot abort evaluation of other rules or channels.
classifications.validation: Enqueue failures are logged with event and channel identifiers; successfully accepted notification:send tasks run on the critical queue with MaxRetry(3). There is no claimed notification-delivery repair path, so outbound delivery remains explicitly best-effort.

classifications.reason: This direct enqueue happens inside the recovery sweep for failed template applications. If delivery fails, the durable failed row remains eligible for the next sweep after backoff.
classifications.validation: cluster_template:drift_check is scheduled on the tunnel queue and guards repeated failures with failedApplyMinBackoff.

classifications.reason: The path creates a durable cluster_decommissions operation before dispatch and prefers a deduplicated task_outbox row. If both outbox and direct acceleration are unavailable, the pending operation remains eligible for repair.
classifications.validation: cluster:decommission_all is scheduled every minute on the tunnel queue, re-enqueues pending rows, and the reconciler uses a lease claim plus persisted idempotent phase state; TestGitOpsDecommissionWritesTaskOutboxBeforeDirectEnqueue verifies the normal outbox contract.

classifications.reason: Security scan ingestion is anchored by a durable security_scan_results row. Re-enqueue failures return an error for asynq retry, and timeout updates the durable scan row as failed.
classifications.validation: security:ingest_scan_results has bounded retry/poll behavior through NewSecurityIngestTask and reschedule.
